importance_of_life_domains|WVS_A.name: importance_of_life_domains
importance_of_life_domains|WVS_A.label: Importance Of Life Domains
importance_of_life_domains|WVS_A.description: Perceived importance of major life domains including family, friends, leisure, politics, work, and religion. Items tap conceptually distinct domains rather than a single latent trait.

child_qualities_autonomy_self_expression.name: child_qualities_autonomy_self_expression
child_qualities_autonomy_self_expression.label: Child Qualities Autonomy Self Expression
child_qualities_autonomy_self_expression.description: Emphasis on child qualities reflecting autonomy, creativity, and self-direction such as independence, imagination, and tolerance.

child_qualities_conformity_tradition|WVS.name: child_qualities_conformity_tradition
child_qualities_conformity_tradition|WVS.label: Child Qualities Conformity Tradition
child_qualities_conformity_tradition|WVS.description: Emphasis on child qualities reflecting conformity, obedience, religious faith, and good manners.

child_qualities_prosocial_diligence|WVS_.name: child_qualities_prosocial_diligence
child_qualities_prosocial_diligence|WVS_.label: Child Qualities Prosocial Diligence
child_qualities_prosocial_diligence|WVS_.description: Emphasis on child qualities reflecting thrift, perseverance, and unselfishness.

social_intolerance_outgroups|WVS_A.name: social_intolerance_outgroups
social_intolerance_outgroups|WVS_A.label: Social Intolerance Outgroups
social_intolerance_outgroups|WVS_A.description: Unwillingness to have various social outgroups (e.g., immigrants, people of different race/religion, homosexuals) as neighbors, reflecting prejudice and social distance.

social_intolerance_deviant_behavior|WVS_.name: social_intolerance_deviant_behavior
social_intolerance_deviant_behavior|WVS_.label: Social Intolerance Deviant Behavior
social_intolerance_deviant_behavior|WVS_.description: Unwillingness to have neighbors who engage in stigmatized behaviors such as drug use or heavy drinking.

subjective_wellbeing|WVS_A.name: subjective_wellbeing
subjective_wellbeing|WVS_A.label: Subjective Wellbeing
subjective_wellbeing|WVS_A.description: Self-reported happiness, life satisfaction, and perceived freedom of choice/control over one's life.

voluntary_association_active_membership|.name: voluntary_association_active_membership
voluntary_association_active_membership|.label: Voluntary Association Active Membership
voluntary_association_active_membership|.description: Count of active memberships or participation across various voluntary organizations, reflecting civic engagement and social capital.

voluntary_association_belonging|WVS_A.name: voluntary_association_belonging
voluntary_association_belonging|WVS_A.label: Voluntary Association Belonging
voluntary_association_belonging|WVS_A.description: Count of voluntary organizations to which the respondent reports belonging (binary membership indicator), reflecting associational social capital breadth.

job_scarcity_gender_discrimination|WVS_C.name: job_scarcity_gender_discrimination
job_scarcity_gender_discrimination|WVS_C.label: Job Scarcity Gender Discrimination
job_scarcity_gender_discrimination|WVS_C.description: Endorsement of prioritizing men over women in employment when jobs are scarce, reflecting gender-based labor market attitudes.

job_scarcity_nativist_preference|WVS_C.name: job_scarcity_nativist_preference
job_scarcity_nativist_preference|WVS_C.label: Job Scarcity Nativist Preference
job_scarcity_nativist_preference|WVS_C.description: Endorsement of prioritizing nationals over immigrants in employment when jobs are scarce, reflecting ethnocentric labor market attitudes.

work_ethic|WVS_C.name: work_ethic
work_ethic|WVS_C.label: Work Ethic
work_ethic|WVS_C.description: Belief in the moral and social importance of work, including viewing idleness negatively, work as a societal duty, and prioritizing work over leisure.

gender_role_traditionalism|WVS_D.name: gender_role_traditionalism
gender_role_traditionalism|WVS_D.label: Gender Role Traditionalism
gender_role_traditionalism|WVS_D.description: Belief that men are superior to women in public roles (politics, business, education) and that women's employment harms children, reflecting traditional gender ideology.

female_income_threat_to_marriage|WVS_D.name: female_income_threat_to_marriage
female_income_threat_to_marriage|WVS_D.label: Female Income Threat To Marriage
female_income_threat_to_marriage|WVS_D.description: Perception that a wife earning more than her husband causes relational problems, reflecting traditional gender-economic norms.

familial_duty_obligations|WVS_D.name: familial_duty_obligations
familial_duty_obligations|WVS_D.label: Familial Duty Obligations
familial_duty_obligations|WVS_D.description: Normative beliefs about obligations within the family, including the duty to have children and to care for aging parents.

confidence_in_domestic_institutions|WVS_.name: confidence_in_domestic_institutions
confidence_in_domestic_institutions|WVS_.label: Confidence In Domestic Institutions
confidence_in_domestic_institutions|WVS_.description: Degree of confidence in key domestic governmental, political, and public institutions in Mexico.

confidence_in_civil_society_organization.name: confidence_in_civil_society_organizations
confidence_in_civil_society_organization.label: Confidence In Civil Society Organizations
confidence_in_civil_society_organization.description: Degree of confidence in non-governmental, charitable, and women's organizations.

confidence_in_international_organization.name: confidence_in_international_organizations
confidence_in_international_organization.label: Confidence In International Organizations
confidence_in_international_organization.description: Degree of confidence in international and supranational organizations such as the UN, IMF, World Bank, WHO, WTO, NATO, and ICC.

perceived_corruption|WVS_E.name: perceived_corruption
perceived_corruption|WVS_E.label: Perceived Corruption
perceived_corruption|WVS_E.description: Perceptions of the extent and actors involved in corruption, including bribery and gift-giving to obtain public services.

electoral_integrity|WVS_E.name: electoral_integrity
electoral_integrity|WVS_E.label: Electoral Integrity
electoral_integrity|WVS_E.description: Perceptions of the fairness, cleanliness, and integrity of the electoral process.

postmaterialist_values|WVS_E.name: postmaterialist_values
postmaterialist_values|WVS_E.label: Postmaterialist Values
postmaterialist_values|WVS_E.description: Inglehart's materialist vs. post-materialist value priorities measured through paired forced-choice items.

autocracy_support|WVS_E.name: autocracy_support
autocracy_support|WVS_E.label: Autocracy Support
autocracy_support|WVS_E.description: Preference for non-democratic forms of governance including strongman rule, technocracy, military rule, and theocracy.

democratic_values_importance|WVS_E.name: democratic_values_importance
democratic_values_importance|WVS_E.label: Democratic Values Importance
democratic_values_importance|WVS_E.description: Ratings of how essential various democratic features are for a country's governance.

democratic_system_evaluation|WVS_E.name: democratic_system_evaluation
democratic_system_evaluation|WVS_E.label: Democratic System Evaluation
democratic_system_evaluation|WVS_E.description: Evaluations of the desirability of democracy, its importance, and the current democratic performance in Mexico.

offline_political_participation|WVS_E.name: offline_political_participation
offline_political_participation|WVS_E.label: Offline Political Participation
offline_political_participation|WVS_E.description: Engagement in conventional and unconventional offline political actions such as petitions, boycotts, demonstrations, strikes, and donations.

online_political_participation|WVS_E.name: online_political_participation
online_political_participation|WVS_E.label: Online Political Participation
online_political_participation|WVS_E.description: Engagement in political activities conducted through digital means including searching for political information, e-petitions, and organizing online.

political_information_sources|WVS_E.name: political_information_sources
political_information_sources|WVS_E.label: Political Information Sources
political_information_sources|WVS_E.description: Frequency of using various media sources to obtain political information.

voting_participation|WVS_E.name: voting_participation
voting_participation|WVS_E.label: Voting Participation
voting_participation|WVS_E.description: Self-reported frequency of voting at local and national levels.

science_technology_optimism|WVS_E.name: science_technology_optimism
science_technology_optimism|WVS_E.label: Science Technology Optimism
science_technology_optimism|WVS_E.description: Beliefs about whether science and technology improve life, create opportunities, and make the world better.

economic_ideology|WVS_E.name: economic_ideology
economic_ideology|WVS_E.label: Economic Ideology
economic_ideology|WVS_E.description: Preferences along the economic left-right spectrum including income equality, state vs. private ownership, government responsibility, and competition.

societal_change_attitudes|WVS_E.name: societal_change_attitudes
societal_change_attitudes|WVS_E.label: Societal Change Attitudes
societal_change_attitudes|WVS_E.description: Attitudes toward broad societal changes including less emphasis on work and greater emphasis on technology.

international_organization_knowledge|WVS.name: international_organization_knowledge
international_organization_knowledge|WVS.label: International Organization Knowledge
international_organization_knowledge|WVS.description: Factual knowledge questions about international organizations (UN Security Council, IMF headquarters, WHO mandate).

authoritarian_governance_tolerance|WVS_E.name: authoritarian_governance_tolerance
authoritarian_governance_tolerance|WVS_E.label: Authoritarian Governance Tolerance
authoritarian_governance_tolerance|WVS_E.description: Acceptance of authoritarian measures such as military takeover and religious law interpretation in governance.

religious_belief|WVS_F.name: religious_belief
religious_belief|WVS_F.label: Religious Belief
religious_belief|WVS_F.description: Latent dimension capturing core supernatural and religious beliefs including belief in God, afterlife, heaven, and hell.

religious_practice_and_self_identificati.name: religious_practice_and_self_identification
religious_practice_and_self_identificati.label: Religious Practice And Self Identification
religious_practice_and_self_identificati.description: Behavioral and self-identified religiosity including service attendance, self-described religiousness, and religious affiliation.

religious_exclusivism|WVS_F.name: religious_exclusivism
religious_exclusivism|WVS_F.label: Religious Exclusivism
religious_exclusivism|WVS_F.description: Degree to which individuals privilege their own religion and religious authority over science and other worldviews.

civic_dishonesty_tolerance|WVS_F.name: civic_dishonesty_tolerance
civic_dishonesty_tolerance|WVS_F.label: Civic Dishonesty Tolerance
civic_dishonesty_tolerance|WVS_F.description: Moral permissiveness toward dishonest civic and economic behaviors such as claiming undeserved benefits, fare evasion, tax evasion, stealing, and bribery.

sexual_and_reproductive_morality_permiss.name: sexual_and_reproductive_morality_permissiveness
sexual_and_reproductive_morality_permiss.label: Sexual And Reproductive Morality Permissiveness
sexual_and_reproductive_morality_permiss.description: Tolerance toward behaviors related to sexuality and reproduction including homosexuality, prostitution, abortion, premarital sex, and casual sex.

life_autonomy_morality_permissiveness|WV.name: life_autonomy_morality_permissiveness
life_autonomy_morality_permissiveness|WV.label: Life Autonomy Morality Permissiveness
life_autonomy_morality_permissiveness|WV.description: Moral permissiveness toward personal life-course decisions including divorce, suicide, and euthanasia.

violence_tolerance|WVS_F.name: violence_tolerance
violence_tolerance|WVS_F.label: Violence Tolerance
violence_tolerance|WVS_F.description: Degree of moral justifiability attributed to various forms of interpersonal and political violence.

religion_versus_secularism_orientation|W.name: religion_versus_secularism_orientation
religion_versus_secularism_orientation|W.label: Religion Versus Secularism Orientation
religion_versus_secularism_orientation|W.description: Orientation on whether religion or secular reasoning should guide personal and social life, capturing the religion-science and faith-reason tension.

outgroup_trust|WVS_G.name: outgroup_trust
outgroup_trust|WVS_G.label: Outgroup Trust
outgroup_trust|WVS_G.description: Degree of generalized trust toward people outside one's immediate social circle, including strangers and those of different religions or nationalities.

perceived_positive_effects_of_immigratio.name: perceived_positive_effects_of_immigration
perceived_positive_effects_of_immigratio.label: Perceived Positive Effects Of Immigration
perceived_positive_effects_of_immigratio.description: Belief that immigration brings beneficial outcomes such as filling jobs, strengthening cultural diversity, providing refuge, and improving lives of people from poorer countries.

perceived_negative_effects_of_immigratio.name: perceived_negative_effects_of_immigration
perceived_negative_effects_of_immigratio.label: Perceived Negative Effects Of Immigration
perceived_negative_effects_of_immigratio.description: Belief that immigration produces harmful consequences including increased crime, terrorism, unemployment, and social conflict.

multilevel_place_attachment|WVS_G.name: multilevel_place_attachment
multilevel_place_attachment|WVS_G.label: Multilevel Place Attachment
multilevel_place_attachment|WVS_G.description: Sense of belonging and emotional attachment across nested geographic levels from local community to the world.

immigrant_origin_status|WVS_G.name: immigrant_origin_status
immigrant_origin_status|WVS_G.label: Immigrant Origin Status
immigrant_origin_status|WVS_G.description: Objective migration background of the respondent and their parents, indicating whether they or their parents were born in the country or are immigrants.

basic_needs_deprivation|WVS_H.name: basic_needs_deprivation
basic_needs_deprivation|WVS_H.label: Basic Needs Deprivation
basic_needs_deprivation|WVS_H.description: Frequency of experiencing deprivation of basic human needs such as food, shelter, income, and medical care.

neighborhood_disorder_and_crime|WVS_H.name: neighborhood_disorder_and_crime
neighborhood_disorder_and_crime|WVS_H.label: Neighborhood Disorder And Crime
neighborhood_disorder_and_crime|WVS_H.description: Perceived frequency of crime, substance abuse, drug dealing, violence, and other antisocial behaviors in one's neighborhood.

crime_victimization|WVS_H.name: crime_victimization
crime_victimization|WVS_H.label: Crime Victimization
crime_victimization|WVS_H.description: Extent to which the respondent or close family members have been victims of crime in the past year.

precautionary_security_behaviors|WVS_H.name: precautionary_security_behaviors
precautionary_security_behaviors|WVS_H.label: Precautionary Security Behaviors
precautionary_security_behaviors|WVS_H.description: Extent to which people in the respondent's environment adopt protective behaviors against crime such as not carrying cash, avoiding going out at night, or carrying weapons.

socioeconomic_insecurity_worry|WVS_H.name: socioeconomic_insecurity_worry
socioeconomic_insecurity_worry|WVS_H.label: Socioeconomic Insecurity Worry
socioeconomic_insecurity_worry|WVS_H.description: Degree of worry about losing employment or being unable to provide children with a good education.

existential_threat_worry|WVS_H.name: existential_threat_worry
existential_threat_worry|WVS_H.label: Existential Threat Worry
existential_threat_worry|WVS_H.description: Degree of worry about large-scale violent threats including war, terrorism, and civil conflict.

acceptance_of_state_surveillance|WVS_H.name: acceptance_of_state_surveillance
acceptance_of_state_surveillance|WVS_H.label: Acceptance Of State Surveillance
acceptance_of_state_surveillance|WVS_H.description: Degree of agreement with government surveillance measures including video monitoring, email monitoring, and data collection on residents.

freedom_security_tradeoff_perception|WVS.name: freedom_security_tradeoff_perception
freedom_security_tradeoff_perception|WVS.label: Freedom Security Tradeoff Perception
freedom_security_tradeoff_perception|WVS.description: Perceptions about whether most people view freedom and security as complementary or as competing values.

institutional_threat_perception|WVS_H.name: institutional_threat_perception
institutional_threat_perception|WVS_H.label: Institutional Threat Perception
institutional_threat_perception|WVS_H.description: Perceived frequency of police/military intrusion into private life and racist conduct as threats to personal security.

science_skepticism|WVS_I.name: science_skepticism
science_skepticism|WVS_I.label: Science Skepticism
science_skepticism|WVS_I.description: Latent disposition reflecting negative or dismissive attitudes toward science and its relevance in everyday life.
